unit.name: Shared-weight computational graphs and BPTT
unit.description: Explains how gradients are computed in unrolled RNNs and why truncated BPTT is used.
unit.summary: The lecture then explains gradient computation when an RNN has multiple time steps and possibly losses at each step [ts=1321s] [ts=1446s]. In the computational graph, every time step uses the same weight matrices; for intuition, gradients can be computed as if each time step had a copy of W, then summed because W is actually shared [ts=1453s] [ts=1523s] [ts=1551s]. Many-to-many models may sum per-step losses, while many-to-one models may use the final hidden state or pooled hidden states [ts=1475s] [ts=1578s]. Backpropagation through time is this gradient computation through repeated applications of the same weights, but long sequences require storing activations and gradients at many steps, creating GPU memory pressure [ts=1667s] [ts=1691s]. Truncated BPTT fixes a time window, passes the final hidden state forward as the next window’s initialization, and stops gradients across window boundaries [ts=1704s] [ts=1740s] [ts=1751s]. Single-output RNNs still backpropagate through earlier hidden states via upstream gradients from the final loss [ts=1782s] [ts=1819s].

key_points.text: RNN computational graphs reuse the same weights at every time step.
Many-to-many losses can be summed across time steps.
Gradients from different time steps are summed into one update for the shared W.
Backpropagation through time computes gradients through repeated time-step uses of the same weights.
Truncated BPTT carries hidden states forward but cuts gradients between fixed windows.

kp_catalog.name: Shared-weight computational graph
kp_catalog.description: Unrolling an RNN while reusing the same parameters at every time step.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because shared-weight computational graph formalizes how unrolling an RNN while reusing the same parameters at every time step, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Backpropagation through time
kp_catalog.description: Computing gradients through the unrolled RNN and summing contributions to shared weights.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because backpropagation through time formalizes how computing gradients through the unrolled RNN and summing contributions to shared weights, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Truncated BPTT
kp_catalog.description: A fixed-window training approximation that passes hidden states forward but cuts gradient history.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because truncated BPTT clarifies a fixed-window training approximation that passes hidden states forward but cuts gradient history, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
